questions.question: What is the difference between a walk, trail, and path in graph theory?
expectedPoints: Walk: Any sequence of vertices and edges
Trail: A walk with no repeated edges
Path: A walk with no repeated vertices
Simple path: A path with no repeated vertices

questions.question: Explain Euler's theorem for Eulerian circuits and paths, including the necessary and sufficient conditions.
expectedPoints: Eulerian circuit visits every edge exactly once and returns to start
Condition: Connected graph with all vertices having even degree
Eulerian path visits every edge exactly once but doesn't return to start
Condition: Connected graph with exactly two vertices having odd degree
Proof idea: Need even degree to enter and leave vertices equally
